nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 塔帕若斯河
nodes.summary: 塔帕若斯河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联40种鸟类，包括细嘴隐蜂鸟、塔帕諾斯隱蜂鳥、Amazonian trogon等40种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Leaden antwren
nodes.summary: Leaden antwren（Leaden antwren，学名 Myrmotherula assimilis），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于塔帕若斯河，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、气候变化、森林砍伐。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: transamazonica is found along the Amazon from the Amazonas-Pará border east to the mouth of the Tapajós River. The leaden antwren almost exclusively inhabits the understory of tall Cecropia forest on river islands, as long as the understory is composed of many species in addition to Cecropia

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is considered very local in Colombia, uncommon in Peru, and fairly common in the rest of its range. "Human activity has little short-term direct effect on the Leaden Antwren [but in] the longer term, the Leaden Antwren potentially is vulnerable to widespread habitat loss, as might occur through perturbations of the Amazonian hydrological regime stemming from widespread deforestation, dam construction, or global climate change."
